claim: The small kana for coda /y/ is not represented by a full-size イ. The text states there is no dedicated small kana.

all_issues.issue: The small kana for coda /y/ is not represented by a full-size イ. The text states there is no dedicated small kana.
Same issue as line 129: full-size イ used for coda /y/, marked with `lang="ain-Kana"`, which may extend the language tag beyond Ainu-specific orthographic forms.

claim: The small kana for coda /w/ is not represented by a full-size ウ. The text states there is no dedicated small kana.

all_issues.issue: The small kana for coda /w/ is not represented by a full-size ウ. The text states there is no dedicated small kana.

Full-size ウ is used for coda /w/, which is correct per the text, but the use of `lang="ain-Kana"` on a standard Japanese kana character that is not part of the Ainu-specific extended set may be inconsistent with markup intent. Similar instances occur at line 135 (イ).

claim: The small kana for coda /n/ is 𛅧, not the full-size character.

all_issues.issue: The small kana for coda /n/ is 𛅧, not the full-size character.

The full-size katakana ン (U+30F3) is used for coda /n/, which is correct per the text, but the `lang="ain-Kana"` attribute may be misleading since ン is a shared Japanese-Ainu character without distinct Ainu-specific phonetic value in this context. However, this is likely intentional for consistency.
